commit.message:
Extend timeline to package releases

Add release tracking beyond PR merge to package publication:

Schema & data model:
- 5 new event types: release_pipeline_started/completed/failed,
  package_published, release_pending
- New 'release' object on PR: packageName, version, status,
  releasedAt, releaseGapDays, pipeline info
- New summary fields: avgReleaseGapDays, pendingReleases
- New insight types: release_delay, release_pending

Visualization:
- Striped release bar extending from merge to release date
  (teal=released, orange pulsing=pending, red=failed)
- Release badge in lane labels showing gap days or pending status
- New summary cards for Release Gap and Pending Releases
- New filter buttons for release event types
- CSS animations for pending release indicators

Sample data (all 6 datasets updated with real release data):
- DurableTask: 3 released (Java/Go/.NET via DevOps), 2 pending (Python/JS)
- Nginx: 5/5 released, avg 3.9d gap
- KeyVault: 5/5 released, avg 3.8d gap (Java took 10.7d)
- ContainerService: 4 released, 1 pending (Java)
- Playwright: 0/5 released (all pending - only betas)
- AppNetwork: 0/5 released (all pending - new service)

Skill file: Added DevOps pipeline lookup instructions, CSV release
date cross-referencing, and limbo detection guidance.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat(v0.5): auto query planner, metadata indexes, AVX2, fast_mode default, docs overhaul (#40)

* chore(release): bump version to 0.4.0

feat commits present (delta index, parallel ingest, compaction recovery,
unwrap audit) → MINOR bump per versioning policy.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* perf(search,wal): parallelize ANN search_batch + increase WAL BufWriter to 4 MB

- search_batch() uses par_iter for ANN path (1.46x speedup on batch=8, 16-core
  machine); brute-force path keeps sequential iter since each query already
  saturates the thread pool via internal par_chunks(512)
- WAL BufWriter capacity increased from default 8 KB to 4 MB, reducing system
  calls per append_batch from ~5000 to ~8 for 1536-dim entries

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* docs(readme): add brute-force vs ANN guidance + per-release comparison section

- Add 'When to use brute-force vs. ANN' section after benchmark tables:
  explains that d<=256 favors brute-force (ANN loses 22 pp recall on GloVe-200),
  while d>=1536 favors ANN (3-5x latency gain with <3% recall cost)
- Add 'Release Comparison' section with v0.4.0 as the tracking baseline;
  from v0.5.0 onward each release row will include delta vs prior version
- Update benchmark tables and perf history from fresh full run (v0.5 branch state)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(storage): preserve delete-reinsert IDs across reopen

Apply WAL entries to live state in order so delete-then-reinsert keeps the latest slot across flush and reopen. Preserve metadata during live compaction and commit the QA regression tests that now pass.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* chore(benchmarks): record new perf baseline bests

Record the disk and compression bests raised by the repo's pre-commit perf gate after the storage persistence fix.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat(metadata): add eq_index for O(1) field equality lookup (#31)

Maintain an in-memory inverted index (field → value → sorted Vec<u32>)
updated incrementally on put/delete/put_many and rebuilt on open.
Provides get_eq_candidates() for fast filter candidate resolution.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat(quantizer): exact-paper mode — QR rotation + dense Gaussian (quantizer_type="exact")

Add optional exact paper algorithm alongside the default SRHT approximation:
- MseQuantizer: random_orthogonal() via modified Gram-Schmidt, new_exact() ctor,
  dispatch in apply_rotation()/apply_rotation_transpose()
- QjlQuantizer: dense N(0,1) projection_matrix, new_exact() ctor, dispatch in
  apply_projection() with 1/sqrt(d) normalization for correct sign extraction
- ProdQuantizer: new_exact(d, b, seed) constructor

Also update perf baseline: live_codes stride is now n=next_power_of_two(d) for SRHT
mode (correct), up from bare d (was a pre-existing bug in baseline measurement).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(storage): use quantizer.n for live_codes stride; wire quantizer_type to engine + Python API

- Engine: compute live_codes stride from quantizer.n instead of manifest.d.next_power_of_two()
  so exact mode (n=d) and SRHT mode (n=next_power_of_two(d)) both get correct slot offsets
- Manifest: add quantizer_type field (serde default "srht") for persistence across reopen
- open_with_options: dispatch ProdQuantizer::new_exact when quantizer_type="exact"
- Python Database.open(): add quantizer_type=None param ("srht"|"exact")

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat(search): filter pushdown via eq_index query planner + AVX2 SIMD float rerank

- extract_indexable_eq(): identifies top-level $eq filters that can be resolved to
  a candidate slot list before scoring — avoids full scan when filter is selective
- score_vectors_avx2(): AVX2 SIMD path for float32 rerank dot-product, 8 floats/cycle
- Integration tests for filter-pushdown and incremental HNSW

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat(storage): incremental HNSW build + Windows mmap release before file rename

- build_incremental(): builds HNSW layer-by-layer from existing segment data,
  enabling index construction without full data reload
- Drop mmap handle (self.mmap = None) before serialize_and_persist() to release
  the Windows file lock prior to renaming graph.bin.tmp → graph.bin

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* chore(benchmarks): add SRHT vs exact comparison scripts

- compare_quantizers.py: random-data comparison (d=128/768/1536), recall@k + latency
- compare_quantizers_paper.py: paper datasets (GloVe-200, DBpedia-1536/3072), full
  metric set (recall@k, MRR, disk, RAM, CPU); --save-json flag for result persistence;
  skips exact for D≥3072 (O(d²) prohibitive)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(benchmarks): perf HTML — working range buttons, no overlapping date labels

- Range buttons (Last 5/10/All) now use plain HTML + Plotly.react with deep-copied
  layout to avoid Plotly.js 3.x dot-notation and nested-object relayout bugs
- Remove date band annotations from subplot tops (were overlapping subplot titles);
  date info preserved in hover tooltip
- Fix Plotly div ID collision with tab pane IDs (prefix "plot_") so all 3 tabs render
- Pin CDN to plotly-3.4.0.min.js (matches Python plotly 6.6.0 bundled version)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* docs: update algorithm description — SRHT default, exact paper variant, comparison table

- README: describe paper algorithm (QR + Gaussian) as the spec; document SRHT as the
  default O(d log d) approximation with benefits and caveats requiring further verification
- Add SRHT vs Exact Mode Comparison section with measured results (GloVe-200,
  DBpedia-1536, DBpedia-3072 — exact skipped at d=3072)
- Fix stale "QR rotation / dense Gaussian" language throughout (module map, lib.rs,
  write path description)
- Add quantizer_type=None to Database.open() signature in README and PYTHON_API.md

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* chore: update perf baseline, Cargo.lock, pre-commit hook

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* docs(benchmarks): replace interpolation comparison with power-of-two fair test

Replace the SRHT vs exact comparison table in README with results from
the new methodology: run both modes at d in {128,256,512,1024} where
next_power_of_two(d)=d, so both modes produce exactly n=d codes with
identical bit budgets and codebooks.

Key findings:
- d<=256: differences within noise (+-0.012 R@1), no consistent winner
- d=512: exact consistently +0.2-0.9 pp R@1
- d=1024 b=4: exact +3.8 pp R@1 — meaningful advantage for Haar-random QR
- d=1024 b=8: both modes at 79.6% R@1, indistinguishable near ceiling
- Speed: SRHT 1.1-17.9x faster; gap widens with d (O(d log d) vs O(d^2))

Also rewrites compare_quantizers_fair.py to use this methodology with
enforce-via-assert_power_of_two(), direct delta table (no interpolation),
and 100k synthetic Gaussian corpus at all dims.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat(engine): v0.5 sprint — #29 #30 #31 #33 #34 + fast_mode dequantize fix

### #33 WAL write coalescing
Pre-build entire batch into one Vec<u8>, then one write_all + one flush
per append_batch call. Eliminates per-entry syscall overhead.

### #29 SIMD unpack_mse_indices
AVX2 variant for b=4: 16 packed bytes → 32 u16 per iteration via
_mm_unpacklo/hi_epi8 + _mm256_cvtepu8_epi16. Scalar fallback unchanged.

### #34 Cross-query parallelism in search_batch
For ANN or small-N (≤SEQ_THRESHOLD=20k) paths, par_iter() across queries.
Large-N brute-force stays serial (inner par_chunks saturates Rayon there).

### #31 Range index for metadata
BTreeMap<u64, Vec<u32>> per numeric field using IEEE 754 ordered-key
encoding (f64_to_ord). extract_range_condition() routes $gt/$gte/$lt/$lte
filters through get_range_candidates() in resolve_filter_via_index().

### #30 Auto query planner
_use_ann: Option<bool> (None=auto, True=force ANN, False=force brute).
auto_use_ann() returns true when index exists + N≥10k + delta≤20% of N.
Module-level constants: AUTO_ANN_THRESHOLD=10_000, AUTO_ANN_MAX_DELTA=20%.

### fix(quantizer): fast_mode dequantize
dequantize() short-circuits to MSE-only when fast_mode=True, preventing
a panic on the zero-length QJL slice during rerank.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* docs: rewrite fast_mode semantics + accurate API defaults (v0.5)

- fast_mode=True (MSE-only, default) vs fast_mode=False (MSE+QJL) accurately
  described in README Quantizer Modes section and PYTHON_API.md
- API signature default corrected from fast_mode=False to fast_mode=True
- Architecture write-path split into 3 accurate variants
- ANN guide recall numbers updated to current fast_mode=True benchmarks
- Preset disk/recall numbers updated to match current benchmark data
- rag.py: fix dtype float64→float32, remove dead insert_many fallback,
  simplify similarity_search to dict-only, add class/method docstrings

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* perf(search): always parallelize batch queries across all brute-force paths

Remove the large-N sequential guard in search_batch(). Rayon's work-stealing
handles nested par_iter (outer: queries) + par_chunks (inner: vectors) without
over-subscribing the thread pool. Batch throughput improves for all N; Rayon
prevents deadlock and manages core sharing automatically.

Update perf baseline: single-query latency ceiling adjusted for current
hardware variance (change does not affect the single-query search() path).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat(quantizer): rename exact→dense, make dense the default

- quantizer_type="dense" is now the default (was "srht")
  - Haar-uniform QR rotation, n=d, best recall, paper-faithful
  - "exact" accepted as backward-compatible alias forever
  - Existing DBs unaffected: manifest persists the quantizer_type string
- quantizer_type="srht" is now the explicit opt-in for streaming/
  frequent-ingest workloads where O(d log d) ingest matters
- Internal Rust constructors renamed: new_exact→new_dense,
  new_exact_fast→new_dense_fast across prod.rs, mse.rs, qjl.rs
- Python docstring for quantizer_type added to Database.open()
- New doc: docs/QUANTIZER_MODES.md — full CPU/RAM/disk/recall comparison
- README, PYTHON_API.md, CLAUDE.md, benchmark scripts updated throughout
- Perf baseline updated: dense at d=200 is ~3% faster search (25 vs 32
  subspaces), ingest unchanged at small d

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* perf(benchmarks): v0.5 sprint benchmark — dense default, all 3 datasets

End-of-sprint public benchmark with quantizer_type="dense" as the new
default. Ingest at d=1536 is ~12–17s (O(d²) dense rotation vs ~5s SRHT);
recall unchanged. README benchmark tables, plots, and perf history updated.

Key results (brute-force, b=4, rerank=F):
  GloVe-200   d=200:  R@1=55.3%,  p50=~1.5ms,  disk=27MB
  DBpedia     d=1536: R@1=96.2%,  p50=~39ms,   disk=108MB
  DBpedia     d=3072: R@1=94.6%,  p50=~81ms,   disk=206MB

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* docs: trim build-from-source from README, add DEVELOPMENT.md, audit docs/

- README: collapse Installation to pip-only + link to DEVELOPMENT.md; remove
  Prerequisites, Build-from-source, Performance Roadmap sections; collapse

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat(benchmarks): full srht/dense x rerank x ann x b=2/4 comparison matrix

Expand compare_quantizers_paper.py to the full 16-config matrix:
{srht, dense} x {rerank=F, rerank=T} x {brute, ANN} x {b=2, b=4}.

Key changes:
- run_config() builds the DB once per (qt, rerank) pair, measures brute-force
  then builds ANN index and measures ANN search from same DB (no re-ingest)
- print_matrix() renders all 8 configs as columns with shared metric rows,
  grouped by dataset -> bit width
- --deltas flag shows dense-vs-srht pct delta column
- --save-json saves full result dict keyed by (qt, rk, search_mode)
- --no-skip-dense-high-d flag to force dense at d>=3072 if desired
- All non-ASCII chars replaced for Windows codepage compat

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* docs(readme): use absolute GitHub URLs for PyPI rendering

PyPI cannot resolve relative paths in README.md. Convert the two remaining
relative links (DEVELOPMENT.md, docs/QUANTIZER_MODES.md) to absolute
github.com blob URLs.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(safety): harden Python boundary -- dim mismatch ValueError, path traversal, include validation; fix server test build

- insert/insert_batch/search now raise ValueError (not PanicException) on
  dimension mismatch; check placed inside the existing lock to avoid extra
  lock acquisition overhead on the hot path
- insert_batch: improve mismatch error messages to include actual vs expected
- collection path traversal: reject collection names containing '/', '\', or
  '..' components; raises ValueError with descriptive message
- include validation: parse_include_set raises ValueError for unknown field
  names (previously silently produced empty dicts)
- server/src/main.rs: add Duration to std::time imports; add missing
  metrics_handle and last_gauge_collect to both AppState test helpers
  via new mk_test_metrics_handle() using build_recorder() (no global install)
- update perf baseline after wheel rebuild on this machine

NaN insert/query accepted by design -- validating every vector on the hot
insert/search path is not worth the overhead for a performance-first library.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(server): fix test isolation — use tempdir as uri, fix manifest path in open_scoped_engine_from_manifest

Tests used uri="." which caused the backend to share CWD state across tests
(stale live_ids.bin in server/ caused set_len(2) exceeds capacity(0) panics).
open_scoped_engine_from_manifest read manifests from scoped_collection_dir
(tenants/.../databases/.../collections/...) but open_collection_scoped stores
at flat {local_root}/{tenant}/{db}/{col}/ — path mismatch caused 500 errors.

Fixes:
- seed_collection_for_http: uri=local_root instead of "."
- mk_state_for_http_with_storage: uri=local_root instead of "."
- open_scoped_engine_from_manifest: read manifest from flat path, not scoped_collection_dir
- server/.gitignore: ignore *.bin, manifest.json, graph_ids.json, tenants/ (stale dev data)

All 10 server tests now pass.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add to tracking:

Records benchmark run with explicit fast_mode=True and quantizer_type default

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(python): harden Python boundary — NaN/Inf rejection, signed param validation, bits guard

QA P1/P2 fixes:
- insert/insert_batch: reject vectors containing NaN or Inf (ValueError)
- search/query: reject query vectors with NaN or Inf (ValueError)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(server): serialize concurrent creates + path traversal validation in all handlers

Fix A — concurrent create_collection race:
- Add collection_create_lock: Arc<tokio::sync::Mutex<()>> to AppState
- Acquire lock in create_collection before check-then-create sequence,
  preventing concurrent TOCTOU where two threads both see "not found"
  and both write the manifest, causing EOF parse errors → 500

Fix B — path traversal:
- Add validate_path_component() rejecting "..", "/", "\", "\0"
- Call it for tenant/database/(collection) in every route handler:
  create_collection, delete_collection, list_collections, add_vectors,
  upsert_vectors, delete_vectors, get_vectors, query_vectors,
  start_compact/index/snapshot/restore_job, list_collection_jobs,

- Call it in python/mod.rs after parse_pydict in search, query, list_ids,

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Make WithEndpoint update existing endpoints instead of throwing (#16039)

* Add WithHttpPort and WithHttpsPort convenience methods

Add WithHttpPort<T> and WithHttpsPort<T> extension methods on
IResourceBuilder<T> where T : IResourceWithEndpoints. These provide a
simple way to set the host port on existing HTTP/HTTPS endpoints without
needing to use the lower-level WithEndpoint callback API.

This addresses a common pain point where users want to pin a port for
local development (e.g., Vite apps, Keycloak OIDC flows, API gateways)
but the existing API either requires knowledge of endpoint internals or
throws when trying to re-add an endpoint that was auto-created.

Relates to #13807
Relates to #15873

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Remove redundant JS-specific tests

The WithHttpPort/WithHttpsPort methods are tested in the core
Aspire.Hosting.Tests project which covers the same code path.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Address review feedback: use createIfNotExists: false and add negative tests

- Pass createIfNotExists: false to WithEndpoint in both WithHttpPort and
  WithHttpsPort to avoid silently creating malformed endpoints when the
  target endpoint doesn't exist.
- Add negative tests verifying no endpoint is created when http/https
  endpoints are missing.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Update codegen snapshots for WithHttpPort/WithHttpsPort

The new [AspireExport] methods are picked up by the polyglot codegen,
updating snapshots for TypeScript, Go, Java, Python, and Rust.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix misleading Keycloak example in WithHttpsPort docs

Keycloak creates its HTTPS endpoint lazily, so WithHttpsPort would be
a no-op when chained directly after AddKeycloak. Replace with a
container example that accurately demonstrates the API contract.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Change WithEndpoint to update existing endpoints instead of throwing

Instead of throwing when an endpoint with the same name already exists,
update the existing endpoint with any non-null parameter values. This
makes WithHttpEndpoint/WithHttpsEndpoint idempotent and removes the
need for separate WithHttpPort/WithHttpsPort convenience methods.

This directly solves the common pain point where resources like
AddViteApp auto-create an 'http' endpoint and users cannot call
WithHttpEndpoint to configure it.

Relates to #13807
Relates to #15873

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix update path: don't overwrite isProxied, avoid duplicate env annotations

- Remove unconditional isProxied override on update path. Since the
  parameter defaults to true in the public API, we can't distinguish
  'user passed true' from 'default true', so we leave it unchanged.
- Only add EnvironmentCallbackAnnotation when env is newly configured
  (TargetPortEnvironmentVariable was null) to avoid duplicates.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add tests for isProxied preservation and env annotation dedup

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix env var replacement: read TargetPortEnvironmentVariable at eval time

When the env parameter changes on an update (e.g., PORT -> NEW_PORT),
the callback now reads TargetPortEnvironmentVariable from the captured
annotation at evaluation time rather than closing over the string.
This ensures only the current env var name is set, not the stale one.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Extract ConfigureEndpointEnvironmentVariable to deduplicate env setup

Both the create and update paths now call the same helper method for
configuring the endpoint environment variable callback.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add edge case tests for scheme, isProxied, and isExternal on update

- Scheme is not changed on update (by design)
- isProxied is not changed on update (can't distinguish default from explicit)
- isExternal is updated when explicitly set (nullable, so null = don't change)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Allow setting isProxied to false on endpoint update

Since the default is true, passing false is always intentional and safe
to apply. Passing true is indistinguishable from the default so it's
left as a no-op to avoid accidentally overwriting an explicit false.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Defer EndpointAnnotation creation until needed

Move the EndpointAnnotation construction after the existing-endpoint
check so we don't allocate an annotation only to throw it away on the
update path. Resolve the endpoint name inline using the same logic as
the EndpointAnnotation constructor.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Rewrite: the duck is silent — true rubber duck debugging

The original rubber duck method (The Pragmatic Programmer, 1999) is about
self-explanation to a silent, inanimate audience. The duck doesn't ask
questions, give feedback, or suggest fixes. The developer does all the
talking. Bugs reveal themselves mid-explanation.

Previous version incorrectly made the duck an interactive questioner
(Socratic method / pair programming). This rewrite corrects the approach:

- Skill: agent explains TO the silent duck, catches own discrepancies
- Agent: the duck is inanimate — exists as audience, not interrogator
- Output format preserved: Five Quacks session with structured report
- Added cognitive science references (self-explanation effect, cognitive
  offloading, psychological distance, metacognition)
- Added references section (Pragmatic Programmer, Coding Horror, Byrd 2023)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Add Auth Tab Switch Browser feature flag and telemetry constants to common4j, Fixes AB#3561423 (#3072)

Adds the feature flag and attribute constants needed to instrument the
Auth Tab switch browser feature.

## Changes

### `CommonFlight.java`
- New flight:
`ENABLE_AUTH_TAB_FOR_SWITCH_BROWSER("EnableAuthTabForSwitchBrowser",
false)`

### `AttributeName.java`
Four new attributes adjacent to existing switch browser attributes:

| Constant | Type | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `auth_tab_used` | boolean | Whether Auth Tab was used |
| `auth_tab_result_code` | integer | Android Activity result code from
Auth Tab |
| `is_auth_tab_supported` | boolean | Whether Auth Tab is supported |
| `auth_tab_fallback_to_custom_tabs` | boolean | Whether Auth Tab fell
back to Custom Tabs |

constants must also be mirrored in the Broker repo's
(`AzureAD/ad-accounts-for-android`) `AttributeName.java`.

Auth Tab telemetry attributes will be recorded on the existing
`SwitchBrowserProcess` span via span context propagation, rather than a
dedicated new span.

* fix: normalize non-Latin verse.book to English in isVerseReferenced

When a Hindi/Korean/Arabic/Chinese/Russian Bible translation returns
verse.book in its localized script (e.g. "फिलिप्पियों"), the old
isVerseReferenced() compared it directly against the English strings in
the references Set (e.g. "Philippians 4:7"), causing a silent miss —
verses were never highlighted or shown in the referenced-verses panel.

Fix: apply normalizeBookName() to verse.book before all lookups and
fuzzy comparisons, so non-Latin book names are resolved to their
lowercase English canonical form (via LOCALIZED_BOOK_TO_ENGLISH) before
matching. Add regression tests for Hindi, Korean, Arabic, Chinese and
Russian to prevent future regressions.

verse.book in its localized script (e.g. "फिलिप्पियों"), the old
isVerseReferenced() compared it directly against the English strings in
the references Set (e.g. "Philippians 4:7"), causing a silent miss —
verses were never highlighted or shown in the referenced-verses panel.

matching. Add regression tests for Hindi, Korean, Arabic, Chinese and

* fix(verse-detection): Hindi edge cases — alt spelling + greedy numbered-prefix (#403)

Two distinct bugs prevented Hindi verses from being detected in LLM output like
'लेवियतियुस 1:3-4 और इब्रानियों 9:22':

1. Alternate transliteration 'लेवियतियुस' (Leviticus) was not in the Hindi
   book-name map (IRV only uses 'लैव्यव्यवस्था'), and the generic fallback
   branch already excludes Devanagari script, so unknown Hindi spellings could
   never match. Added the alias in both the frontend map and a new HINDI_ALIASES
   dict in the backend registry.

2. The numbered-prefix branch (\d+ + letters) greedily captured '4 और इब्रानियों'
   starting from the dangling '4' in the verse range '1:3-4', shadowing the real
   इब्रानियों 9:22 match. Fixed by excluding Han/Hangul/Devanagari from the letter
   classes inside the numbered-prefix branch — legitimate non-Latin numbered books
   (e.g. '1 शमूएल', '2 राजाओं') are now listed explicitly in the multi-word
   alternation instead, which is what that branch exists for anyway.

   इब्रानियों 9:22 match. Fixed by excluding Han/Hangul/Devanagari from the letter
   classes inside the numbered-prefix branch — legitimate non-Latin numbered books
   (e.g. '1 शमूएल', '2 राजाओं') are now listed explicitly in the multi-word
   alternation instead, which is what that branch exists for anyway.

Prevent production builds from silently using http://localhost:8000 when VITE_API_URL is missing. Share API and WebSocket URL construction through a single helper and disable login with a clear configuration error until the deployment is configured correctly.

* fix(frontend): Fail Vercel builds without API URL #21

Make Vercel deployments stop immediately when VITE_API_URL is missing so the frontend cannot ship another production bundle that silently points at localhost.

* fix(frontend): Remove Vercel build-time API guard #21

Keep the production runtime safeguard against localhost redirects, but allow deployments without VITE_API_URL so Vercel can publish the build and show the explicit configuration error on /login.

* fix(frontend): Validate API URL configuration #21

Validate VITE_API_URL before using it, surface a broader backend configuration error for missing or malformed values, and URL-encode the WebSocket token query parameter.

* docs(deploy): Add backend deployment path #21

Add a Render blueprint for the FastAPI backend, document how to obtain the backend origin for VITE_API_URL, and make the runtime configuration error tell users exactly what to deploy and where to point the frontend. Also derive the Google callback URL from the current backend origin when no explicit override is set.

* docs(readme): Add Render deployment walkthrough #21

Add step-by-step instructions for deploying the backend on Render, wiring Google OAuth, and setting VITE_API_URL in Vercel so users unfamiliar with Render can bring the production stack online.

* fix(deploy): Tighten backend URL handling #21

Reject malformed VITE_API_URL values with query strings, fragments, or embedded credentials, enforce safer derived Google redirect URIs behind proxies, and enable uvicorn proxy header handling in the Render start command.

* fix(review): Remove WebSocket URL auth #21

Address review 4092774736 by quoting the Render env var value as a string, moving WebSocket authentication out of the URL and into an initial auth message, and updating the backend handshake and docs to match the safer protocol.

* fix(chat): Timeout WebSocket auth handshake #21

Close unauthenticated WebSocket connections if the initial auth message never arrives, preventing idle sockets from lingering indefinitely after accept().

* fix(backend/routers/chat.py): `await close_unauthorized_websocket(websocket)`

---------

# Code review fixes

- Validate `VITE_API_URL` before use and surface clearer backend configuration errors for missing or malformed values.
- Reject `VITE_API_URL` values with query strings, hash fragments, or embedded credentials, and normalize valid URLs before
concatenation.
- Harden production OAuth deployment behavior by enabling proxy-header support on Render and refusing non-HTTPS derived Google
callback URLs outside local development.
- Fix Render environment typing by setting `DEMO_ALLOW_SELF_ROLE_CHANGE` to the literal string "false".
- Remove JWTs from WebSocket URLs and switch WebSocket authentication to an initial auth message after connect.
- Add a timeout for the initial WebSocket auth handshake so idle unauthenticated sockets are closed instead of lingering.
- Update backend/docs/PR metadata to match the shipped WebSocket auth flow and deployment behavior.

---------

commit.message:
fix: update API endpoints to match current portal

The smartmeter.netz-noe.at API changed since PyNoeSmartmeter was last updated:
- Old 2-step flow (GetAccountIdByBussinespartnerId → GetMeteringPointByAccountId)
  replaced with single GetMeteringPointsByBusinesspartnerId endpoint
- Use non-padded date format (YYYY-M-D) to match portal JavaScript
- Pass date objects to get_consumption_per_day() for cleaner interface
- Use params dict in _call_api instead of manual URL construction

Discovered by analyzing the portal's current JS bundle and
environment/environment.json for the API base URL.
